business_goal: Prevent credit exposure on orders by ensuring no customer exceeds their authorized credit threshold

in_scope: Credit limit validation for online and phone orders
Rejection of orders that exceed customer credit limit
out_of_scope: Credit limit modification or adjustment workflows

business_rules.statement: System must reject orders where the order amount exceeds the customer's authorized credit limit
Credit limit is read from CUSTPF using customer ID as key

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Order is rejected with error code 42 when amount exceeds limit
acceptance_criteria.how_to_test: Submit order with amount > customer limit; verify error code 42 returned

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Error message 'Credit limit exceeded' is returned to caller
acceptance_criteria.how_to_test: Verify ERRMSG field contains 'Credit limit exceeded'

acceptance_criteria.criterion: Credit limit is correctly read from CUSTPF customer master file
acceptance_criteria.how_to_test: Query CUSTPF for test customer; verify CRDLIM field matches system value

modernization_decisions.decision: Implement credit check as a reusable Spring Boot service
modernization_decisions.rationale: Allows order entry, billing, and other bounded contexts to call the service independently
modernization_decisions.trade_off: Service boundary adds network hop vs. monolithic implementation
modernization_decisions.impact: Enables independent scaling and testing of credit business logic

modernization_decisions.decision: Use PostgreSQL CUSTMSTR table as the system-of-record for credit limits
modernization_decisions.rationale: CUSTMSTR is the master data source in legacy; preserved for accuracy
modernization_decisions.trade_off: Synchronization required if credit limits are changed outside the system
modernization_decisions.impact: Simplifies data migration; one source of truth maintained

fields.notes: Amount of credit currently in use (open orders + invoices)

inputs.name: Order
inputs.notes: Incoming order request from order-entry service

outputs.name: Credit Decision
outputs.notes: Credit check result; used to accept or reject order

exceptions.condition: Customer record not found in CUSTMSTR
exceptions.expected_behavior: Return error code 99 (customer not found) to caller
exceptions.handling: Service returns 400 BadRequest with error message

exceptions.condition: Order amount is zero or negative
exceptions.expected_behavior: Treat as non-credit order; no limit check needed
exceptions.handling: Service returns approval without credit limit validation

test_cases.title: Order approved when amount is below credit limit
Order rejected when amount exceeds credit limit
Customer not found error
test_cases.notes: Error case; may not have legacy equivalent

open_questions.question: Should the system apply a daily credit limit in addition to the per-order limit?
open_questions.resolution: Deferred to Phase 2 by John Smith, Credit Policy Manager
open_questions.notes: Not in scope for Phase 1; current system has no daily limit; revisit after Go-Live

assumptions.assumption: CUSTPF contains all active and inactive customer records; service will query live
assumptions.rationale: Customer master is System of Record; all credit decisions depend on CUSTPF
assumptions.impact: Downstream service must handle missing customer gracefully (error 99)

assumptions.assumption: Credit limit values in CUSTPF are always valid decimal(9,2)
assumptions.rationale: Observed in all analyzed transactions; DDS confirms numeric(9,2)
assumptions.impact: No currency conversion needed; all comparisons are numeric

sme_sign_offs.name: John Smith
sme_sign_offs.notes: Approved for Atlas SDD chain consumption.
